raw.objective: “to explore the effects of age including the perimenopausal age range on the clearance of” –LMT/OXC (MHD)/CBZ
raw.covariates: Tested: SEX,WT,AGE Included: WT,AGE, SEX on CL; WT on Vd
raw.observations: Due to missing demographics of their population, it is difficult to assess which population this model describes. Clearance seems to peak at age 36, with lower CL on both sides of that age. No goodness of fit plots are presented, which does not allow to interpret the quality of the fit.
raw.equations: Ka=theta1 Vd/F=theta2*WT if(AGE<36) TVCL=theta3*(1+theta4*SEX)*((WT/71)**theta5)*((36/AGE)**theta6) else TVCL=theta3*(1+theta4*SEX)*((WT/71)**theta5)*((AGE/36)**theta6) CL/F=TVCL*exp(eta1)*exp(OCC*eta2) Cobs=Cipred+eps1 OCC: occasion (0 for first, and 1 for second occasion, although the paper does not make clear how occasions are defined)
raw.parameter_values: theta1=0.224 theta2=1.4 theta3=3.59 theta4=1.04 theta5=0.269 theta6=-0.261 omega1=0.0808 omega2=0.006 sigma1=1.6

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending
Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.residual_error: Ka=theta1 Vd/F=theta2*WT if(AGE<36) TVCL=theta3*(1+theta4*SEX)*((WT/71)**theta5)*((36/AGE)**theta6) else TVCL=theta3*(1+theta4*SEX)*((WT/71)**theta5)*((AGE/36)**theta6) CL/F=TVCL*exp(eta1)*exp(OCC*eta2) Cobs=Cipred+eps1 OCC: occasion (0 for first, and 1 for second occasion, although the paper does not make clear how occasions are defined)
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “to explore the effects of age including the perimenopausal age range on the clearance of” –LMT/OXC (MHD)/CBZ

covariates.raw: Tested: SEX,WT,AGE Included: WT,AGE, SEX on CL; WT on Vd

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
